name: Kyoto

palette.name: Kyoto Purple
palette.rationale: Purple is treated as civic-symbol context because Kyoto-affiliated public material describes it as the official color of Kyoto.

palette.name: Kinkaku Gold
palette.rationale: Muted gold references temple ornament and the strong visitor-facing image of gilded architecture.

palette.name: Garden Moss
palette.rationale: Deep moss green reflects Kyoto's garden traditions and shaded temple landscapes.

palette.name: Raked Sand
palette.rationale: A light sand neutral gives dry gardens, stone paths, and temple courtyards a quiet base.

palette.name: Torii Vermilion
palette.rationale: Vermilion is a symbolic shrine accent, useful for Kyoto imagery but not an official city color.

palette.name: Machiya Timber
palette.rationale: Dark timber anchors townhouses, temple structures, and narrow street interiors.

palette.name: Bamboo Shade
palette.rationale: Soft bamboo green adds a calm natural accent for western hills and grove imagery.

sources.title: Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)
sources.note: Used for temple, wooden architecture, garden, and cultural-center context.

sources.title: About the Foundation
sources.note: Used for Kyoto purple as civic-symbol context.

notes: Complete starter record with one civic-symbol color and editorial heritage, garden, architectural, and natural layers.
editorialSummary: Kyoto's starter palette combines civic purple with temple gold, moss, raked sand, vermilion, timber, and bamboo shade for a calm heritage-and-garden design system.
